seoTitle: Get Memo Line to Print on Checks in QuickBooks
metaDescription: How to turn on the check memo preference in QuickBooks Online so your description notes print directly onto physical vendor checks.
intro: Sending a check without an account or invoice number on the memo line often leaves vendors confused about what it's for. QuickBooks Online has a global preference that lets your description text flow through to the printed check stub — you just need to switch it on first.

steps.heading: Open Account and Settings
steps.body: Click the Gear icon and select Account and Settings to reach your company preferences.

steps.heading: Go to the Advanced tab
steps.body: In the left-hand settings menu, click Advanced, then find the Expenses row panel.

steps.heading: Turn on the memo preference
steps.body: Click the Edit pencil and check the box for copying the memo onto check printing layouts.

steps.heading: Save the setting
steps.body: Click Save, then Done, to lock in the global preference.

steps.heading: Test it on a real check
steps.body: Create a new Check or Expense, type a note in the memo box, check Print Later, save it, then run Print Checks to confirm the text appears on the preview.

faq.question: Where do I type the note that will show up on the check?
faq.answer: Type it into the memo field when creating the Check or Expense form — that's the text that gets carried through to the printed stub.

faq.question: Do I need to turn this preference on every time I print?
faq.answer: No, it's a one-time global setting saved in Advanced settings, and it applies to all future checks once activated.

faq.question: Will this work with Standard check stock too?
faq.answer: The memo copy feature is described as working well with voucher check styles specifically, which tend to have more room for text.

faq.question: How do I check the memo shows up correctly before printing?
faq.answer: The print preview screen in the Print Checks dashboard shows a live view where you can confirm the text appears neatly before printing.
